title: Biden faces Israel quandary with new Netanyahu government
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s new government is little more than a week old but it's already giving the Biden administration headaches.Just days into its mandate, a controversial member of Netanyahu’s right-wing Cabinet riled U.S.

title: McCarthy elected House speaker in rowdy post-midnight vote
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Kevin McCarthy was elected House speaker on a historic post-midnight 15th ballot early Saturday, overcoming holdouts from his own ranks and floor tensions that boiled over after a chaotic week that tested the new GOP majority’s ability to govern.

title: Jan. 6 remembrance led by Dems as GOP wrestled with rebels
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden conferred high honors on those who stood against the Jan. 6 Capitol mob two years ago and the menacing effort in state after state to upend the election, declaring “America is a land of laws, not chaos,” even as disarray rendered Congress dysfunctional for a fourth straight day.

title: A look at the final GOP holdouts McCarthy hopes to sway
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — Six GOP members-elect remained opposed to Rep.

title: US to send $3.75B in military aid to Ukraine, its neighbors
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. will send $3.75 billion in military weapons and other aid to Ukraine and its neighbors on NATO's eastern flank, the White House announced Friday, as Russia's invasion of Ukraine grinds on.

title: EPA moves to toughen standards for deadly soot pollution
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration is proposing tougher standards for a deadly air pollutant, saying that reducing soot from tailpipes, smokestacks and wildfires could prevent thousands of premature deaths a year.

title: Ashli Babbitt’s mother arrested during Jan. 6 demonstration
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — The mother of Ashli Babbitt, the woman fatally shot by police inside the U.S. Capitol during the Jan.

title: Lame-duck Illinois lawmakers vote themselves a 16% raise
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — Illinois House Democrats working in a lame-duck session left town for a weekend break Friday night — but not before voting on plans to give themselves a pay raise of nearly 16%.

title: Ex-lawmaker’s final vote questioned after move from Vegas
shortDescription: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Shortly before being appointed as a rural Nevada judge last month over a deep pool of applicants, then-Las Vegas Councilwoman Michele Fiore insisted to county officials that she was eligible for the judgeship because she had met the qualifications to be considered a local resident.

title: US appeals court blocks ban on rapid-fire ‘bump stocks’
shortDescription: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A Trump administration ban on bump stocks — devices that enable a shooter to rapidly fire multiple rounds from semi-automatic weapons after an initial trigger pull — was struck down Friday by a federal appeals court in New Orleans.

title: Illinois House OKs semiautomatic weapons ban, on to Senate
shortDescription: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A ban on dozens of semiautomatic weapons, a top campaign promise of Gov. J.B. Pritzker, won Illinois House approval early Friday. The House voted 64-43 to outlaw .50-caliber guns and cartridges, not defining the term semiautomatic but listing specific types or brands of 20 pistols and five dozen rifles.

title: Montana lawmaker wants to revisit idea of reservations
shortDescription: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A white state lawmaker in Montana is questioning whether land set aside long ago for Native Americans should exist anymore.Republican Sen. Keith Regier is proposing asking Congress to study alternatives to reservations.

title: State investigators to review 2 law enforcement shootings
shortDescription: HUNTSVILLE, Ala. (AP) — The state law enforcement agency is investigating two separate fatal shootings by law enforcement officers.Authorities released limited information about the shootings in Gadsden and in Hazel Green, a community in Madison County.

title: Judge scolds Trump lawyers, denies bid to toss fraud lawsuit
shortDescription: NEW YORK (AP) — A Manhattan judge on Friday scolded Donald Trump’s lawyers as he denied their bid to dismiss a lawsuit accusing the former president and his company of a yearslong fraud scheme.Judge Arthur Engoron said lawyers for Trump and his Trump Organization “should have known better” than to recycle what he said were frivolous, meritless arguments in paperwork seeking the dismissal of New York Attorney General Letitia James’ lawsuit.

title: Guest lineups for the Sunday news shows
shortDescription: WASHINGTON (AP) — ABC’s “This Week” — Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas; Reps. Scott Perry, R-Pa., and Andy Barr, R-Ky.; U.S. Capitol Police Officer Harry Dunn.

title: Shootings in Albuquerque share target: elected Democrats
shortDescription: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Bullets flew through one home's front door and garage. At another home, three bullets went into the bedroom of a 10-year-old girl in a series of shootings that had at least one thing in common: They all targeted the homes or offices of elected Democratic officials in New Mexico.

title: Ex-Kentucky governor gives speech, skips filing for old job
shortDescription: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Former Kentucky Gov. Matt Bevin added an element of drama to the governor's race Friday, teasing a bid for the Republican nomination before leaving the state Capitol without jumping into the state's top political race as the filing period expired for the May primary.

title: Minnesota governor still wants tax rebates from huge surplus
shortDescription: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz said Friday that his budget proposal will include tax rebates from the state's enormous $17.6 billion budget surplus but conceded that the payments will be smaller that he once hoped.

title: Wisconsin Republicans try to skirt vetoes, let voters decide
shortDescription: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Republican lawmakers are moving quickly in the opening days of the legislative session to work around Wisconsin's Democratic Gov. Tony Evers, who vetoed a record number of bills from the GOP-controlled Legislature in his first term.

title: Montana man gets nearly 4 years for role in US Capitol riot
shortDescription: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man who was among the first people to illegally enter the U.S. Capitol while Congress was certifying President Joe Biden’s victory in the 2020 election was sentenced to nearly four years in federal prison on the two-year anniversary of the insurrection.

title: W.Va. leadership discusses taxes, education ahead of session
shortDescription: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — Public education, health insurance for government employees and restructuring the ailing Department of Health and Human Resources will be key priorities for West Virginia lawmakers during the new legislative session beginning next week.

title: Message from US asylum hopefuls: Financial sponsors needed
shortDescription: MIAMI (AP) — Migrants and asylum seekers fleeing violence and persecution in their home countries have increasingly found that protections in the United States are available to those with money or the savvy to find someone to vouch for them financially.

title: Michael Flynn restored to Twitter on Jan. 6 anniversary
shortDescription: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Former national security adviser Michael Flynn had his Twitter account reinstated on Friday as the United States marked the two-year anniversary of the Jan. 6 insurrection.Twitter suspended the account of the retired Army lieutenant general two days after the violent assault on the capitol.

title: South Carolina US House district ruled racial gerrymander
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Federal judges ordered South Carolina lawmakers to draw new congressional maps, ruling Friday that the U.S. House district lines of a seat flipped by Democrats four years ago were intentionally redrawn to split Black neighborhoods to dilute their voting power.

title: SC governor’s budget seeks pay bumps to shore up workforce
shortDescription: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster wants to increase 2022's record amount of capital investment and offset shortages across workforces like education and law enforcement, according to the budget request he released Friday.

title: UN to vote on aid deliveries to Syria’s rebel-held northwest
shortDescription: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council is set to vote Monday on a resolution that would continue humanitarian aid deliveries to Syria’s rebel-held northwest from Turkey for another six months, and all eyes will be on Russia.

title: New Pa. speaker puts sexual abuse lawsuit window atop agenda
shortDescription: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's new state House speaker declared Friday that no other legislation will be taken up by his chamber until it approves a constitutional amendment granting child sexual abuse victims the power to file what would otherwise be outdated claims.

title: ‘Don’t Say Gay’ feud advances with new proposed legislation
shortDescription: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida lawmakers will move to increase state control of Walt Disney World's private government, according to a notice published Friday, the latest development in a feud over a law critics have dubbed “Don't Say Gay.”

title: Ex-lawmaker who served time for Jan. 6 riot seeks House seat
shortDescription: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — Two years to the day after storming the U.S. Capitol, a former West Virginia state lawmaker who served prison time for his role in the riot said Friday that he hopes to return to the scene of his crime as an elected official.

title: Michigan AG pledges to pursue case against Trump electors
shortDescription: DETROIT (AP) — The Michigan attorney general said Friday there's "clear evidence" to pursue charges against pro-Donald Trump Republicans who claimed they were the state's presidential electors in 2020, despite Democrat Joe Biden's 154,000-vote victory.

title: US appeals court blocks ban on rapid-fire ‘bump stocks’
shortDescription: A Trump administration ban on bump stocks was blocked by an appeals court. The device was banned after used by the gunman in the 2017 Las Vegas attack

title: Live updates: On marathon 15th ballot, McCarthy wins House speaker vote
shortDescription: On a marathon fourth day, after a chaotic week for House Republicans, GOP Leader Rep. Kevin McCarthy won speaker of the House.

title: Biden honoring those who showed courage during attack on Capitol
shortDescription: President Biden will bestow the nation's second highest civilian honor on a dozen people who stood up to attempts to overturn the 2020 election.

title: 13 House GOP members received campaign money from McCarthy but voted against him
shortDescription: Embattled speaker of the House hopeful Kevin McCarthy gave to 13 of the same people who are voting repeatedly to keep him from taking the gavel.

title: 'WTF is wrong with him?': How Jan. 6 shook loyalties of Trump aides
shortDescription: On Jan. 6, 2021, some Donald Trump aides sought to get him to denounce violence, others were frustrated by his failure to abide by election results.

title: Arrest of El Chapo's son results in Mexican cartel violence before Biden visit
shortDescription: Days before Biden's trip to Mexico, the country spiraled into violence after the arrest of Ovidio Guzmán, son of infamous drug lord known as "El Chapo."

title: Biden says Mexico will step up help on border
shortDescription: Ahead of his anticipated border trip, Biden said Mexico will accept the return of undocumented migrants from Venezuela, Cuba, Haiti and Nicaragua.

title: Rep. Cammack jabs Democrats during speaker vote
shortDescription: Rep. Kat Cammack riled up Democrats after accusing them of bringing "popcorn and blankets and alcohol" to vote on a new House speaker.

title: Rep. Crenshaw condemns GOP opposition to McCarthy
shortDescription: Rep. Dan Crenshaw criticized the Republicans voting against Rep. Kevin McCarthy for speaker, saying that 'they are enemies now.'

title: George Santos nearly misses call for speaker vote
shortDescription: Rep.-elect George Santos was skipped during a Congressional vote for a new speaker of the House after he failed to respond to multiple roll calls.

title: Recap: House speaker vote fails again, impasse breaks 100-year record
shortDescription: The vote for a new speaker of the House dragged into day three Thursday as Republican leader Rep. Kevin McCarthy lost a series of ballots.

title: OnPolitics: As House speaker vote drags on, what can be done?
shortDescription: After nine rounds of voting, Rep. Kevin McCarthy still doesn't have the votes for House speaker. What happens now?

title: Here are the 12 people being honored by Biden on the Jan. 6 anniversary
shortDescription: Biden is giving one of the nation's highest civilian honors to 12 people who showed "courage and selflessness" related to the Jan. 6 attacks.

title: Meet Byron Donalds, the GOP representative nominated for House speaker
shortDescription: Republican opponents to GOP Rep. Kevin McCarthy as House speaker nominated Florida Rep. Byron Donalds for speaker of the House as voting continues.

title: Biden to visit the border for the first time
shortDescription: President Joe Biden unveiled new steps aimed at stemming historic migration as he plans to visit El Paso, Texas.

title: What happens after McCarthy loses speaker votes? The process explained
shortDescription: Candidates for speaker are nominated from the floor by members of their parties. The speaker who is chosen does not have to be a member of Congress.

title: House speaker election creates viral moments
shortDescription: Members of Congress voiced frustration as GOP members continue to battle over who should be the next speaker of the House.

title: Jan. 6 attack 2 years later: Trump still plagued by multiple investigations
shortDescription: Two years after the deadly Capitol attack, Trump’s potential legal exposure has only grown more perilous, with his future political viability unknown.

title: Biden and McConnell appear together, striking contrast to Kevin McCarthy House chaos
shortDescription: McConnell hailed a $1.6 billion bridge project for Kentucky and Ohio a legislative miracle.' Biden said it proves "we can work together."

title: In Child Welfare Cases, Most of Your Constitutional Rights Don’t Apply
shortDescription: The child welfare system rarely offers the same rights as the criminal justice system, leaving many families facing permanent separation without due process protections.

title: Patients Went to This Isolated Facility for Treatment. Instead, Nearly Two Dozen Were Charged With Crimes.
shortDescription: For years, residents at the Illinois facility received scant treatment for their developmental disabilities and mental illnesses, then faced felonies for lashing out at staff.

title: The Moment When Tempers Really Got Out of Control on the House Floor
shortDescription: Tensions flared when Representative Matt Gaetz sunk Kevin McCarthy’s 14th effort to be elected speaker of the House.By Luke Broadwater

title: After Dramatic 14th Vote, Trump Calls Holdouts Who Refused to Back McCarthy
shortDescription: The former president made crucially timed calls to Representative Matt Gaetz and others who sunk McCarthy’s 14th effort to be elected House speaker.By Maggie Haberman

title: A Confluence of Forces: Anti-Kevin McCarthy and Pro-2020 Election Lies
shortDescription: Some of the same Republicans who were former President Donald J. Trump’s lieutenants in seeking to overturn the 2020 election led the charge against Representative Kevin McCarthy as speaker.By Luke Broadwater

title: Why Does the House Keep Repeating the Same Vote Over and Over?
shortDescription: Until the House chooses a speaker, it is legally unable to do anything else. As long as it is in session, it has to vote for a speaker. If the vote fails, it has to vote again. And again.By Maggie Astor

title: McCarthy Wins Speakership on 15th Vote After Concessions to Hard Right
shortDescription: An agreement with ultraconservative Republicans delivered a breakthrough for the California Republican, but he still had to claw his way to the post during a dramatic post-midnight session.By Annie Karni

title: How the last freshman holdout against McCarthy made it to Congress
shortDescription: Eli Crane, newly elected from Arizona, is the only first-term Republican still opposing Kevin McCarthy for speaker in the latest vote.
